name: Alphabet Inc.
lede: Google runs nine centers across seven Indian cities. Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Pune, New Delhi, Gurugram, Mumbai and Visakhapatnam, covering GBS, R&D and IT, built over 22 years.
insight: Search elsewhere and you will usually find one Google office in India. We map nine, across seven cities, each tagged by center type and function. Knowing the whole footprint is what turns a name on a list into an account you can plan against.
metaTitle: Alphabet Inc. GCC in India: Centers, Footprint & Leaders | Bamboo Reports
metaDescription: Google runs 9 verified centers across 7 Indian cities (Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Pune, New Delhi, Gurugram, Mumbai, Visakhapatnam), covering GBS, R&D and IT. See the full India footprint, org map and contacts on Bamboo Reports.

titles.title: Director, Ads Marketing and Brand & Reputation

about.description: Google's India footprint covers Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Pune, New Delhi, Gurugram, Mumbai and Visakhapatnam, spanning GBS, R&D and IT. The centers deliver IT, Finance & Accounting, HR and Procurement for the group's global operations.

bandCopy: Exact center locations, per-center headcount, the technology stack and all five decision-makers, in one profile.

faq.q: How many centers does Google operate in India?
faq.a: Google operates 9 active centers in India. Eleven are on record in total, including non-operational sites.

faq.q: Which Indian cities does Google have centers in?
faq.a: Google's India footprint includes Bengaluru and 6 additional cities.

faq.q: What types of centers does Google run in India?
faq.a: Google runs GBS and 2 additional center types centers in India.

faq.q: How many people work at Google's India centers?
faq.a: Aggregate and center-level headcount for Google's India operations is available in the full profile.

faq.q: Who leads Google's GCC in India?
faq.a: Bamboo Reports tracks leaders at Google's India centers, across Finance and other leadership functions. Names and contact details open with a free account.

sources: company filings, LinkedIn, NASSCOM and Bamboo Reports' primary research
